qwen.label: Qwen3-Coder-Next
qwen.notes: High-volume codegen/refactors/tests. Weak on terminal tasks (Terminal-Bench 36.2%). Cerebras route dropped (served older 480B, below Sonnet-4.5 level); Qwen3.8-Max noted for future (closed preview, no public API yet).

kimi.label: Kimi K3
kimi.notes: 1M ctx deep reasoning (~Opus tier). Expensive, slow (~34 tok/s), always-on thinking billed as output. SiliconFlow fallback is independent infra (OpenRouter just relays to Moonshot), same $3/$15.

deepseek.label: DeepSeek V4-Flash
deepseek.notes: Cheapest of the fleet (~Haiku tier). SiliconFlow hosts the same V4-Flash ($0.13/$0.28, pricier than OR $0.09/$0.18).

deepseek-pro.label: DeepSeek V4-Pro
deepseek-pro.notes: DeepSeek flagship (1.6T/49B active). Claims SWE-bench 80.6% (vendor, unverified). Cheaper than GLM via OpenRouter; strong price/quality for demanding codegen.

grok.label: Grok 4.5
grok.notes: xAI's latest (Jul 2026). Frontier coding/STEM generalist, good second-opinion model. OpenRouter only in v1.

glm.label: GLM-5.2
glm.notes: Best independently verified of the fleet (SWE-bench Pro 62.1%, #1 open-weight). Multi-step refactors, tool-use style output (~Sonnet 5 tier). SiliconFlow ID verified ($1.302/$4.092, pricier than OR).
